summary.en: Mexican multinational cement producer. Founded 1906 in Hidalgo, Mexico; transformed by CEO Lorenzo Zambrano (1985-2014) from a regional cement company into the world's #3 cement and #1 ready-mix concrete producer. The case is famous in business-model literature not for the commodity (cement) but for the *delivery service overlay* — Cemex industrialised concrete dispatch using GPS, telematics, FedEx-style logistics, and a 20-minute delivery commitment in cities where competitors needed half a day. The customer 'job' shifted from 'buy concrete' to 'have concrete on-site exactly when needed' — a Jobs-To-Be-Done reframing that created a service moat around a commodity product. Construrama (2001) extended the model into a retailer-network programme that brought thousands of small Mexican building-supply stores under a single brand + supply-chain operation. The case is intentionally NOT tagged with a BMG pattern — its primary teaching value is operational excellence + JTBD reframing, not any of the five patterns currently shipped in this library.
summary.zh: 墨西哥跨国水泥生产商。1906 年创立于墨西哥伊达尔戈;CEO Lorenzo Zambrano(1985-2014 任期)把它从一家区域性水泥公司转型为全球第 3 大水泥商、第 1 大预拌混凝土商。这个案例在商业模式文献中出名,不是因为水泥本身这个大宗商品,而是因为**服务叠加层** —— Cemex 用 GPS、车载终端、FedEx 式调度,把混凝土配送工业化,在竞争对手需要半天的城市做到「20 分钟送达」承诺。客户的「任务」从「买混凝土」变成「让混凝土准时到工地」 —— 这是「Jobs-To-Be-Done」的重新构型,在大宗商品周围造出一条服务护城河。Construrama(2001)把这一模式延展为零售商网络计划,把数千家墨西哥小型建材店纳入同一个品牌 + 供应链运营。本案例**有意不**打 BMG 模式标签 —— 它的核心教学价值是运营卓越 + JTBD 重新构型,不是本库目前承载的五个模式中的任何一个。

sources.label: Pankaj Ghemawat · 'The Globalization of CEMEX' · Harvard Business School Case 9-701-017 · 2000
Adrian Slywotzky · The Profit Zone · Crown Business · 1997 (CEMEX as JTBD example)
